hypothesis: Incorporating DFT+U corrections specifically calibrated for 3d transition metal MOFs as an intermediate fidelity level in a multi-fidelity ∆-learning cascade (GFN1-xTB → PBE+U → HSE06) will yield greater predictive accuracy for band gaps of transition-metal-containing MOFs than the two-level GFN1-xTB → HSE06 scheme, because the DFT+U correction explicitly addresses the strong correlation effects that cause GFN1-xTB to systematically underestimate band gaps in Cr, Mn, Fe, Co, and Ni MOFs, thereby reducing the magnitude and variance of the ∆ that the machine learning model must learn.
